title: crataegus monogyna var. eriocarpa
intro: Crataegus monogyna var. eriocarpa, also known as the hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species within the Crataegus monogyna variety. The medicinal properties of Crataegus monogyna var. eriocarpa are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins, and other bioactive compounds. This plant has been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health, lower blood pressure, and improve circulation, thereby reducing the risk of heart disease and stroke. In herbal preparations, the leaves, flowers, and berries of Crataegus monogyna var. eriocarpa are often used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules that can be consumed orally to harness its health benefits.
